stages.concept:
# Unit 104: 감각동사 + 형용사 - 개념톡

👩‍🏫 선생님: 오늘은 **감각동사 + 형용사**에 대해 배워볼까요?

> The cake **smells** delicious.
> 그 케이크는 맛있는 냄새가 난다.

👩‍🏫 선생님: 감각동사는 오감(시각, 청각, 촉각, 미각, 후각)과 관련된 동사예요. 뒤에 **형용사(보어)**가 와요.

👩‍🏫 선생님: **look** — ~하게 보이다 (시각)

> She **looks** tired.
> 그녀는 피곤해 보인다.

👩‍🏫 선생님: **sound** — ~하게 들리다 (청각)

> That **sounds** great!
> 그거 좋게 들린다!

👩‍🏫 선생님: **feel** — ~하게 느끼다 (촉각)

> This blanket **feels** soft.
> 이 담요는 부드럽게 느껴진다.

👩‍🏫 선생님: **taste** — ~한 맛이 나다 (미각)

> This soup **tastes** salty.
> 이 수프는 짠맛이 난다.

👩‍🏫 선생님: **smell** — ~한 냄새가 나다 (후각)

> The flowers **smell** wonderful.
> 그 꽃들은 멋진 향기가 난다.

**Q. 다음 문장의 빈칸에 들어갈 알맞은 단어를 고르세요.**
"The music sounds (       )."
1. beautifully
2. beautiful ✅
3. beauty

👩‍🏫 선생님: 감각동사 뒤에는 한국어 해석과 상관없이 **부사가 아니라 형용사**가 와야 한다는 점을 절대 잊지 마세요!

> ❌ She looks beautifully.
> ✅ She **looks** beautiful.

🧑‍🎓 학생: look/sound/feel/taste/smell 뒤에는 형용사! 부사가 아니라 형용사를 쓴다는 것에 주의해야겠어요.
stages.practiceA:
# Unit 104: 감각동사+형용사 - 연습문제 A

**01.** 아래 문장에서 틀린 부분을 선택하고, 맞게 고쳐쓰세요.
The cake tastes sweetly.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** sweetly → sweet

---

**02.** She looks beautifully today.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** beautifully → beautiful

---

**03.** The music sounds nicely.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** nicely → nice

---

**04.** This blanket feels softly.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** softly → soft

---

**05.** The soup smells deliciously.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** deliciously → delicious

---

**06.** 보기 안에서 알맞은 말을 고르시오.
This flower smells (     ) .
> **정답** good

---

**07.** She looks ? today.
> **정답** happy

---

**08.** The song sounds ? .
> **정답** beautiful

---

**09.** The bread tastes ? .
> **정답** sweet

---

**10.** This pillow feels ? .
> **정답** soft

---

**11.** 우리말에 맞게 빈칸에 알맞은 말을 쓰시오.
이 꽃은 좋은 냄새가 난다.
This flower smells .
> **정답** good

---

**12.** 그녀는 슬퍼 보인다.
She looks .
> **정답** sad

---

**13.** 그 노래는 아름답게 들린다.
The song sounds .
> **정답** beautiful

---

**14.** 이 과일은 달콤한 맛이 난다.
This fruit tastes .
> **정답** sweet

---

**15.** 이 천은 부드럽게 느껴진다.
This cloth feels .
> **정답** soft

---

**16.** 우리말에 맞게 다음 단어들을 바르게 배열하시오.
이 음식은 맛있는 냄새가 난다.
This food delicious smells
> **정답** This / food / smells / delicious

---

**17.** 그는 피곤해 보인다.
looks He tired
> **정답** He / looks / tired

---

**18.** 그 피아노 소리는 아름답게 들린다.
The piano beautiful sounds
> **정답** The / piano / sounds / beautiful

---

**19.** 이 케이크는 달콤한 맛이 난다.
cake This sweet tastes
> **정답** This / cake / tastes / sweet

---

**20.** 이 이불은 따뜻하게 느껴진다.
blanket This warm feels
> **정답** This / blanket / feels / warm
stages.practiceB:
# Unit 104: 감각동사+형용사 - 연습문제 B

**01.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 수 없는 것을 고르시오.
The dog looks ____________________.
lovely
cutely
tired
friendly
> **정답** cutely

---

**02.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 수 없는 것을 고르시오.
Coffee from this cafe tastes
________________________.
sweet
strangely
good
delicious
> **정답** strangely

---

**03.** 다음 밑줄 친 부분이 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
The pizza smelled so well.
A woman on the bus looked upset.
The kid's voice sounded cheerful.
The medicine tasted bitter.
> **정답** The pizza smelled so well.

---

**04.** 다음 밑줄 친 부분이 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 고르시오.
[2개]
The food at the restaurant tasted salty.
Tom felt like a thief when he picked a coin
on the street.
My sister looks happiness when she can go to
bed early.
My dad's voice sounded a stranger that night.
> **정답** My dad's voice sounded a stranger that night., My sister looks happiness when she can go to bed early.

---

**05.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을
고르시오.
Tom's lunch looked ____________________,
but he didn't feel _________________.
delicious - hungry
delicious - hungrily
deliciously - hungry
deliciously - hungrily
> **정답** delicious - hungry

---

**06.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을
고르시오.
Her voice sounded ________________.
She also looked so __________________.
strangely - tiring
strange - tired
strangely - tiredly
strangely - tired
> **정답** strange - tired

---

**07.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을
고르시오.
The muffins I baked yesterday __________________
strawberries.
My mom loved them and I __________________
proud and happy.
tasted like - felt like
tasted - felt like
tasted like - felt
tasted - felt
> **정답** tasted like - felt

---

**08.** 다음 중 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
The dish tasted nice.
I felt comfortably in the chair.
Amy looked interested in the activities.
The cake smelled good.
> **정답** I felt comfortably in the chair.

---

**09.** 다음 중 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
Tom felt scared to see her.
The test looked hardly.
That sounded perfect.
Her bag looked costly.
> **정답** The test looked hardly.

---

**10.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
그녀는 어른처럼 보이지만, 목소리는 아기 같다.
She looks like an adult, but sounds a baby.
She looks an adult, but sounds a baby.
She looks an adult, but sounds like a baby.
She looks like an adult, but sounds like a
baby.
> **정답** She looks like an adult, but sounds like a baby.

---

**11.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
나는 배가 고파서 케이크를 먹었다. 그러나 그 케이크는 너무
달았다.
I felt very hungry, so I ate the cake. But the
cake tasted too sweet.
I felt very hungrily, so I ate the cake. But the
cake tasted too sweet.
I felt very hungrily, so I ate the cake. But the
cake tasted too sweetly.
I felt very hungry, so I ate the cake. But the
cake tasted too sweetly.
> **정답** I felt very hungry, so I ate the cake. But the cake tasted too sweet.

---

**12.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
그는 매우 아프고 창백해 보였다. 마치 귀신 같아 보였다.
He looked very sick and pale. He looked a
ghost.
He looked very sickly and pale. He looked a
ghost.
He looked very sick and pale. He looked like a
ghost.
He looked very sickly and palely. He looked
like a ghost.
> **정답** He looked very sick and pale. He looked like a ghost.

---

**13.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
This cake tasted deliciously.
Amy looked nice in the dress.
He sounded angrily.
The man on the street looked like hungry.
> **정답** Amy looked nice in the dress.

---

**14.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
Tom looked like happy about the news.
The teacher's voice sounded serious.
These cookies taste chocolate.
This soup smelled strangely.
> **정답** The teacher's voice sounded serious.

---

**15.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
That sounds like perfect.
Amy's muffins tasted banana.
Amy's pencil case looked costly.
She felt weakly when she was sick.
• weakly 힘없이
• costly 많은 돈[비용]이 드는
> **정답** Amy's pencil case looked costly.

---

**16.** 어법상 틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오.
When I gave him a gift, he
looked happily.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** happily. → happy

---

**17.** 어법상 틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오.
Cakes at this cafe always taste
too sweetly.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** sweetly. → sweet

---

**18.** 어법상 틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오.
Tom sounded seriously after he
went to hospital.
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
> **정답** seriously → serious

---

**19.** 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 어색한 것을 골라 고쳐 쓰시오.
[2개]
I am an only child, so I often feel lonely . So I
often invite my friends. Today, my friends came to
my house and we baked cookies together. They
looked delicious . We gave them to my mom.
She said they tasted wonderfully . She didn't
sound honest , so we tried them ourselves.
They tasted terribly .
> **정답** lonely, delicious, wonderful, honest, terrible

---

**20.** 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 어색한 것을 골라 고쳐 쓰시오.
[2개]
Amy's family went to a restaurant. They ordered a
lot of dishes. But, all their dishes tasted salt .
Amy called the cook and told him about it. The
cook looked upset . Amy felt sorry , but
she asked him to cook again. When the new dishes
arrived, all the food looked fantastic. They tasted
well , too.
> **정답** salty, upset, sorry, good
stages.descriptive:
# Unit 104 감각동사+형용사중1 (중등) - 서술형문제

**01.** 우리말에 맞게 문장을 완성하시오.
Andrew는 그녀에게 친절하게 보였다. [look, kind]
Andrew  (     )   (     )  to her.
정답 looked, kind

---

**02.** 우리말에 맞게 문장을 완성하시오.
그녀의 계획은 완벽하게 들렸다. [perfect]
Her plan  (     )   (     ) .
정답 sounded, perfect

---

**03.** 우리말에 맞게 문장을 완성하시오.
그 바나나머핀은 바나나 맛이 나지 않았다. [bananas]
The banana muffins didn't  (     )   (     )   (     ) .
정답 taste, like, bananas

---

**04.** 주어진 말을 이용하여 질문에 대답하는 문장을
완성하시오.
look, kind
A: What does your new homeroom teacher look like?
B: Well, she  (     )   (     ) .
정답 looks, kind

---

**05.** 주어진 말을 이용하여 질문에 대답하는 문장을
완성하시오.
taste, awful
A: Was your lunch delicious?
B: No, it  (     )   (     ) .
• awful 끔찍한
정답 tasted, awful

---

**06.** 주어진 말을 이용하여 질문에 대답하는 문장을
완성하시오.
feel, better
A: How did you feel after the treatment, Molly?
B: I  (     )  a lot  (     )  after the treatment.
정답 felt, better

---

**07.** 어법상 어색한 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
[Jane]  [felt  ] [painfully]  [when]  [she]
[fell]  [down.]
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
정답 painfully → painful

---

**08.** 어법상 어색한 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
[The]  [sushi]  [tasted]  [terrible,]  [so]  I
[felt]  [unhappily.]
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
정답 unhappily. → unhappy

---

**09.** 어법상 어색한 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
[This]  [sweater]  [feels]  [smoothly.]
틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요. (생략가능)
정답 smoothly. → smooth

---

**10.** 주어진 감각동사를 활용하여 다음 글을 완성하시오.
(시제에 유의할 것)
look / feel / taste / smell
Early in the morning, my mom was roasting meat in
the kitchen. It  (     )  nice. I  (     )  happy to eat
meat. But, she said it was for dinner. I complained
and it sounded a bit rude. My mom  (     )  upset
because of my behavior. At last, she let me have a
bite. It  (     )  delicious.
• complain 불평하다
• behavior 행동
정답 smelled (smelt), felt, looked, tasted

---
stages.practical:
# Unit 104: 감각동사+형용사 - 변형문제

**01.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 수 없는 것을 고르시오.
The cat looks ____________________. lovely cutely sleepy friendly
> 정답: cutely

---

**02.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 수 없는 것을 고르시오.
Tea from this shop tastes ________________________. sweet strangely good wonderful
> 정답: strangely

---

**03.** 다음 밑줄 친 부분이 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
The bread smelled so well.
A man on the train looked worried.
The girl's voice sounded bright.
The medicine tasted sour.
> 정답: The bread smelled so well.

---

**04.** 다음 밑줄 친 부분이 어법상 틀린 것을 모두 고르시오. [2개]
The food at the cafeteria tasted bland.
Tom felt like a spy when he hid behind the door.
My brother looks happiness when he can play games.
My mom's voice sounded a stranger that morning.
> 정답: My mom's voice sounded a stranger that morning., My sister looks happiness when she can go to bed early.

---

**05.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을 고르시오.
Amy's dinner looked ____________________, but she didn't feel _________________.
delicious - hungry delicious - hungrily deliciously - hungry deliciously - hungrily
> 정답: delicious - hungry

---

**06.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을 고르시오.
His voice sounded ________________.
He also looked so __________________.
strangely - tiring strange - tired strangely - tiredly strangely - tired
> 정답: strange - tired

---

**07.** 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말이 순서대로 알맞게 짝지어진 것을 고르시오.
The cookies I baked yesterday __________________ vanilla.
My dad loved them and I __________________ proud and happy.
tasted like - felt like tasted - felt like tasted like - felt tasted - felt
> 정답: tasted like - felt

---

**08.** 다음 중 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
The soup tasted nice.
I felt comfortably on the sofa.
Tom looked interested in the project.
The pie smelled good.
> 정답: I felt comfortably on the sofa.

---

**09.** 다음 중 어법상 틀린 것을 고르시오.
Tom felt scared to watch the movie.
The quiz looked hardly.
That sounded wonderful.
Her shoes looked costly.
> 정답: The quiz looked hardly.

---

**10.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
그는 어른처럼 보이지만, 목소리는 아이 같다.
He looks like an adult, but sounds a child.
He looks an adult, but sounds a child.
He looks an adult, but sounds like a child.
He looks like an adult, but sounds like a child.
> 정답: He looks like an adult, but sounds like a child.

---

**11.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
나는 목이 말라서 주스를 마셨다. 그러나 그 주스는 너무 셨다.
I felt very thirsty, so I drank the juice.
But the juice tasted too sour.
I felt very thirstily, so I drank the juice.
But the juice tasted too sour.
I felt very thirstily, so I drank the juice.
But the juice tasted too sourly.
I felt very thirsty, so I drank the juice.
But the juice tasted too sourly.
> 정답: I felt very thirsty, so I drank the juice. But the juice

---

**12.** 다음 우리말을 가장 바르게 영작한 것을 고르시오.
그녀는 매우 아프고 창백해 보였다. 마치 유령 같아 보였다.
She looked very sick and pale.
She looked a ghost.
She looked very sickly and pale.
She looked a ghost.
She looked very sick and pale.
She looked like a ghost.
She looked very sickly and palely.
She looked like a ghost.
> 정답: She looked very sick and pale. She looked like a ghost.

---

**13.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
This pie tasted deliciously.
Amy looked nice in the outfit.
He sounded angrily.
The woman on the bus looked like hungry.
> 정답: Amy looked nice in the outfit.

---

**14.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
Tom looked like upset about the result.
The coach's voice sounded firm.
These crackers taste chocolate.
This stew smelled strangely.
> 정답: The coach's voice sounded firm.

---

**15.** 다음 중 어법상 맞는 것을 고르시오.
That sounds like amazing.
Amy's pancakes tasted honey.
Tom's backpack looked costly.
She felt weakly when she was ill.
> 정답: Tom's backpack looked costly.

---

**16.** 어법상 틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오. When  I  gave  her  a  gift,  she looked  happily. 틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요.
(생략가능)
> 정답: happily. → happy

---

**17.** 어법상
틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오. Cookies  at  this  bakery  always  taste too  sweetly. 틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요.
(생략가능)
> 정답: sweetly. → sweet

---

**18.** 어법상
틀린 곳을 찾아 바르게 고쳐쓰시오. Amy  sounded  seriously  after  she went  to  the  doctor. 틀린 부분만 고쳐 쓰세요.
(생략가능)
> 정답: seriously → serious

---

**19.** 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 어색한 것을 골라 고쳐 쓰시오. [2개]
I am an only child, so I often feel lonely .
So I often invite my friends.
Today, my friends came to my house and we made sandwiches together.
They looked delicious .
We gave them to my dad.
He said they tasted wonderfully .
He didn't sound honest , so we tried them ourselves.
They tasted terribly .
> 정답: lonely, delicious, wonderful, honest, terrible

---

**20.** 밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 어색한 것을 골라 고쳐 쓰시오. [2개]
Tom's family went to a restaurant.
They ordered many dishes.
But, all their dishes tasted salt .
Tom called the waiter and told him about it.
The waiter looked upset .
Tom felt sorry , but he asked the chef to cook again.
When the new dishes arrived, all the food looked fantastic.
They tasted well , too.
> 정답: salty, upset, sorry, good
